qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unrestricted environment variable injection via PowerLine token-writer enables code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets arbitrary environment variable injection in PowerLine token writing, not the advisory's missing authContext workspace scoping in knowledge_search and knowledge_get_node.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: knowledge_search and knowledge_get_node are scoped tools whose handlers omit authContext enforcement, allowing a scoped agent to supply another workspaceId and read cross-workspace knowledge data.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-647h-p824-99w7 names packages/mcp/src/tools/knowledge.ts knowledge_search and knowledge_get_node plus packages/mcp/src/tool-scoping.ts SCOPED_TOOLS as the affected code.
The final qualifying finding env-var-injection-token-writer names packages/powerline/src/token-writer.ts and token creation/push paths as the affected code.
The session input manifest lists env-var-injection-token-writer as the only final qualifying vulnerability folder.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was reported, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
